commits.author: Matteo Greco
commits.subject: test(ETC-656): guard ENABLE_AI_USE_MIDDLEWARE stays off in production flags
commits.body:
Fails CI if config/flags.json accidentally flips the middleware provider
on before the feature is ready to ship. Delete the assertion when ETC-656
lands.

commits.author: Matteo Greco
commits.subject: refactor(ETC-656): extract AiProviderFactory and cover flag routing
commits.body:
Moves the AI provider selection out of AiRoutes::__construct into an
AiProviderFactory that takes both provider instances and a predicate via
constructor injection. AiRoutes now calls AiProviderFactory::default(),
which is the only place that reads the ENABLE_AI_USE_MIDDLEWARE flag.

Adds three focused unit tests that assert the dispatch branches without
touching global flag state: predicate-true returns middleware,
predicate-false returns openai, and the predicate is re-evaluated on each
create() call.

Adds `ai.provider` to the AI wide event on both providers so the activity
log unambiguously shows which path ran when the ENABLE_AI_USE_MIDDLEWARE
flag flips. MiddlewareProvider also tags `ai.middleware.url`.

ETC-656.title: I can send “hello world” and get a streaming response back
ETC-656.description:
Let's front-load the work by getting the risky part out of the way: 

Let's get the plugin, the middleware and Open AI to communicate

**Technical decisions:**

* The endpoint on the middleware side is `/assistant` (already created)
* A feature flag must be created in the plugin
* The feature flag is OFF by default
* We send a dummy message ("hello"), not the actual user message
* The middleware injects something in the message so we can identify it came through the middleware

**Acceptance criteria:**

- [X] Given the feature flag is OFF, the plugin contacts Open AI directly
- [X] Given the feature flag is ON, I can see a response from Open AI through the middleware being streamed in chunks
- [X] If I don't receive a response from Open AI under 30 seconds, the middleware sends back an error to the plugin stating the issue
- [X] If Open AI responds back with an error, the error is forwarded to the plugin
- [X] If the middleware responds back with an error, the plugin displays it.

**Findings**

* from svelte we receive message
* from php, we take the messages, we grab the api key, we send to middleware
* from middleware to php, we reuse the streaming, we reuse the events types, and it goes back to svelte
* on middleware receives messages and api key
* sends to open ai
* receives stream

ETC-666.title: Component properties panel redesign
ETC-666.description:
## 🔖 Summary

Our new component properties panel has to support new JSON editor functionality. Because of that, the props panel header design needs a slight re-design.

![image.png](https://uploads.linear.app/48a03f57-ea0e-4dba-b596-2bf09659f4d1/4a5b7343-a7ee-4fb3-bcc8-0e9c47d781cf/ba80e2fd-6b02-4c24-aae3-48535c2cb5c1)

## 📗 Acceptance Criteria

1. When previewing a component instance, user can see:
   * detach component instance icon
   * edit component icon
2. When inside the component editor mode, user can see:
   * a dev switch for the JSON editor
   * a "Format JSON" icon when the JSON editor is enabled
   * a key icon for showing prop keys
   * a plus icon for adding component props
3. Each action icon in the `Component properties` header has a descriptive tooltip (e.g. Key icon = "Show property keys")
4. User sees an actual `component name` instead of "Component editor" label
5. `Component name` turns into a `component key` when the user enables "Show property keys" icon button
6. User no longer sees the purple border around the props
7. User no longer sees the component description (unnecessary for now)
8. User no longer sees a `toggle preview` icon

ETC-591.title: Break up `AiChatContent.svelte` — God component
ETC-591.description:
# Acceptance criteria:

- [X] All behaviors expressed by `AiChatContent.svelte` are test covered **before** refactoring
- [X] The refactor breaks up the God component into smaller, separate components
- [X] `AiChatContent.svelte` and all created files have CodeScene health 10 after the refactor
- [X] All tests pass after the refactor

# Code smell analysis

**Change frequency:** 1 commit (new), but inherits the 32-commit hotspot pattern from `AiChatContent.svelte`

**Smells found:**

1. **Mixed concerns in one file** — The component handles: (a) message list rendering, (b) user input + keyboard shortcuts, (c) image paste/drop handling, (d) CSS/HTML insert/replace action logic, (e) toast notifications, (f) \~600 lines of CSS. These are distinct responsibilities that will change for different reasons.
2. **Duplicated action handler pattern** — `handleInsertCssFromSegment`, `handleInsertHtmlFromSegment`, `handleReplaceHtmlFromSegment` all follow the same try/catch/toast pattern with minor variations. Three functions with identical structure.
3. **\~600 lines of CSS** — The markdown styling (headings, code blocks, tables, blockquotes, scrollbars, buttons) is a standalone concern duplicated from the old component. It styles `AiChatSegmentsRenderer` and `AiChatCodeSegment` children via `:global()`, which makes it hard to tell which styles belong to which child.

**Suggested refactoring:**

* **Extract the input area** into a separate `AiChatInputArea.svelte` component. It has its own state (`textareaElement`, `messagesContainer`), its own events (keydown, paste, drop, dragover), and its own rendering (mode switch, pending images, textarea, send button). \~120 lines of script + \~80 lines of CSS. This is the most impactful extraction because input handling and message rendering change for different reasons.
* **Extract markdown CSS** into a shared stylesheet or a wrapper component (e.g. `AiChatMarkdownStyles.svelte` that just wraps `<slot />` with the styles). This removes \~400 lines from the component and eliminates the `:global()` reach-through.
* **Safety: Safe** — No tests exist for `AiChatContentNew.svelte` yet (it's behind a flag), but the extractions are mechanical moves that don't change behavior. Once tests are ported from Finding 1, they'll cover the extracted pieces.

ETC-632.title: Redesign Text Prop Input
ETC-632.description:
### Summary

Implement vertically stacked layout and other small UI changes for component's `Text Prop` input.

![image.png](https://uploads.linear.app/48a03f57-ea0e-4dba-b596-2bf09659f4d1/e4824536-915a-4c50-b5da-56551a3742f8/fea39fcd-6b5b-4c5a-9530-33233113dfd0)

### Acceptance Criteria

* The user can see vertically stacked layout for the `Text prop` input in both the component editor mode and when previewing the component instance.
* The user can see `Text Prop Icon` when previewing component instance
* There is a clear indication of what should happen with the `Text prop label` grows too long
* There is a clear indication of what should happen with the `Text prop value` grows too long
* User has the ability to resize the value text field

ETC-652.title: Add Command + Enter keyboard shortcut to "Add Selector Buttons"
ETC-652.description:
Context:
Right now it's a bit hidden that the user can press CMD + Enter to access the "Selector/Attribute Add Menu", to make this more apparent we should add the keyboard shortcut to all the buttons.

AC:

* The user has a visual hint that he can press CMD + Enter to access the selector menu
* The "Add Selector to get started" button has the keyboard shortcut visible

ETC-649.title: Add "shortcut" option to tooltip
ETC-649.description:
## 🔖 Summary

Tooltips should ideally include keyboard shortcuts (e.g., Create a component ⌥+⌘+K). To keep the focus on the primary action, the shortcut should be slightly dimmed because the shortcut itself should serve as a subtle visual guide for the user.

![image.png](https://uploads.linear.app/48a03f57-ea0e-4dba-b596-2bf09659f4d1/9e8b130a-946b-4a81-b052-616c6590e4b1/2d38ccac-2a46-4625-a795-da42013eedb2)

## 📗 Acceptance Criteria

* Users can see the shortcut alongside the descriptive label in the tooltip (in some areas of the builder - where appropriate).
* Shortcut has a slightly lower opacity

ETC-647.title: Remove unnecessary icons from HTML Code editor's header
ETC-647.description:
## 📗 Acceptance Criteria

* User can no longer see the "Duplicate" & "Delete" icons inside the HTML Code editor header

![image.png](https://uploads.linear.app/48a03f57-ea0e-4dba-b596-2bf09659f4d1/93f542b7-86c1-4a75-a9a1-6a343b141989/5508e2a7-e3ef-4215-aed2-034615711fc1)

ETC-643.title: Add "Create component" icon into the "Element Settings" panel
ETC-643.description:
### 🔖 Summary

To improve discoverability and align with UX best practices, the "`Create a component`" action should be moved from the hidden right-click menu to a more prominent and visible location.

![image.png](https://uploads.linear.app/48a03f57-ea0e-4dba-b596-2bf09659f4d1/95718445-f150-417c-9307-8f8e7b4bf38a/60da6462-20ab-4ae8-ae83-cf9ae80f779d)

### 📗 Acceptance Criteria

* The user can see a new "Component icon" in the Element Settings header.
* Hovering that icon shows an explanatory tooltip with "Create a component" text.
* Clicking this icon will transform that element into a component and it will take the user directly in the component editor mode
* Note: "Create component" action should still be visible in the right-click menu as well

ETC-646.title: Default Populator for Loop Prop
ETC-646.description:
**Context:** 
There is currently no way to populate loop prop values with the target click, unlike other prop types (e.g. text).

**Acceptance Criteria:**

* A "target" icon is shown when hovering a loop prop
* Clicking the target icon enters "target mode"
* Clicking the target icon again exits "target mode"
* While in target mode, the user can click a block on the canvas
* On canvas block click, the block is automatically wrapped in an `{#loop}` element with `props.loopProp` as the loop and `item` as the item key, and target mode is exited

ETC-650.title: Persist Prop Accordion States

ETC-645.title: Default Populator for Boolean Prop
ETC-645.description:
**Context:** 
There is currently no way to populate boolean prop values with the target, unlike other prop types (e.g. text).

**Acceptance Criteria:**

* A "target" icon is shown when hovering a boolean prop
* Clicking the target icon enters "target mode"
* Clicking the target icon again exits "target mode"
* While in target mode, the user can click a block on the canvas
* On canvas block click, the block is automatically wrapped in an `{#if}` element with `props.booleanProp` as the condition, and target mode is exited

Bonus: 

* Make it possible to not only click on the canvas but to also allow clicking on the structure panel item

ETC-616.title: Handle copy pasting into style editor
ETC-616.description:
Context:

AC:

* The user can replace the whole style editor content when the pasted selector matches the selector that's already there
* If the selector does not match, or more than one css rule is pasted, the paste is aborted
* The user can still paste within the curly braces without changes or special handling
